speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: Nurse, where’s my daughter? Call her forth to me.

speaker: NURSE
text: Now, by my maidenhead at twelve year old,

speaker: NURSE
text: I bade her come.—What, lamb! What, ladybird!

speaker: NURSE
text: God forbid. Where’s this girl? What, Juliet!

speaker: JULIET
text: How now, who calls?

speaker: NURSE
text: Your mother.

speaker: JULIET
text: Madam, I am here. What is your will?

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: This is the matter.—Nurse, give leave awhile.

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: We must talk in secret.—Nurse, come back again.

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: I have remembered me, thou ’s hear our counsel.

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: Thou knowest my daughter’s of a pretty age.

speaker: NURSE
text: Faith, I can tell her age unto an hour.

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: She’s not fourteen.

speaker: NURSE
text: I’ll lay fourteen of my teeth (and yet, to my teen

speaker: NURSE
text: be it spoken, I have but four) she’s not fourteen.

speaker: NURSE
text: How long is it now to Lammastide?

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: A fortnight and odd days.

speaker: NURSE
text: Even or odd, of all days in the year,

speaker: NURSE
text: Come Lammas Eve at night shall she be fourteen.

speaker: NURSE
text: Susan and she (God rest all Christian souls!)

speaker: NURSE
text: Were of an age. Well, Susan is with God;

speaker: NURSE
text: She was too good for me. But, as I said,

speaker: NURSE
text: On Lammas Eve at night shall she be fourteen.

speaker: NURSE
text: That shall she. Marry, I remember it well.

speaker: NURSE
text: ’Tis since the earthquake now eleven years,

speaker: NURSE
text: And she was weaned (I never shall forget it)

speaker: NURSE
text: Of all the days of the year, upon that day.

speaker: NURSE
text: For I had then laid wormwood to my dug,

speaker: NURSE
text: Sitting in the sun under the dovehouse wall.

speaker: NURSE
text: My lord and you were then at Mantua.

speaker: NURSE
text: Nay, I do bear a brain. But, as I said,

speaker: NURSE
text: When it did taste the wormwood on the nipple

speaker: NURSE
text: Of my dug and felt it bitter, pretty fool,

speaker: NURSE
text: To see it tetchy and fall out with the dug.

speaker: NURSE
text: “Shake,” quoth the dovehouse. ’Twas no need, I trow,

speaker: NURSE
text: To bid me trudge.

speaker: NURSE
text: And since that time it is eleven years.

speaker: NURSE
text: For then she could stand high-lone. Nay, by th’ rood,

speaker: NURSE
text: She could have run and waddled all about,

speaker: NURSE
text: For even the day before, she broke her brow,

speaker: NURSE
text: And then my husband (God be with his soul,

speaker: NURSE
text: He was a merry man) took up the child.

speaker: NURSE
text: “Yea,” quoth he, “Dost thou fall upon thy face?

speaker: NURSE
text: Thou wilt fall backward when thou hast more wit,

speaker: NURSE
text: Wilt thou not, Jule?” And, by my holidam,

speaker: NURSE
text: The pretty wretch left crying and said “Ay.”

speaker: NURSE
text: To see now how a jest shall come about!

speaker: NURSE
text: I warrant, an I should live a thousand years,

speaker: NURSE
text: I never should forget it. “Wilt thou not, Jule?” quoth he.

speaker: NURSE
text: And, pretty fool, it stinted and said “Ay.”

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: Enough of this. I pray thee, hold thy peace.

speaker: NURSE
text: Yes, madam, yet I cannot choose but laugh

speaker: NURSE
text: To think it should leave crying and say “Ay.”

speaker: NURSE
text: And yet, I warrant, it had upon its brow

speaker: NURSE
text: A bump as big as a young cock’rel’s stone,

speaker: NURSE
text: A perilous knock, and it cried bitterly.

speaker: NURSE
text: “Yea,” quoth my husband. “Fall’st upon thy face?

speaker: NURSE
text: Thou wilt fall backward when thou comest to age,

speaker: NURSE
text: Wilt thou not, Jule?” It stinted and said “Ay.”

speaker: JULIET
text: And stint thou, too, I pray thee, nurse, say I.

speaker: NURSE
text: Peace. I have done. God mark thee to his grace,

speaker: NURSE
text: Thou wast the prettiest babe that e’er I nursed.

speaker: NURSE
text: An I might live to see thee married once,

speaker: NURSE
text: I have my wish.

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: Marry, that “marry” is the very theme

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: I came to talk of.—Tell me, daughter Juliet,

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: How stands your disposition to be married?

speaker: JULIET
text: It is an honor that I dream not of.

speaker: NURSE
text: An honor? Were not I thine only nurse,

speaker: NURSE
text: I would say thou hadst sucked wisdom from thy teat.

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: Well, think of marriage now. Younger than you

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: Here in Verona, ladies of esteem,

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: Are made already mothers. By my count

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: I was your mother much upon these years

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: That you are now a maid. Thus, then, in brief:

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: The valiant Paris seeks you for his love.

speaker: NURSE
text: A man, young lady—lady, such a man

speaker: NURSE
text: As all the world—why, he’s a man of wax.

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: Verona’s summer hath not such a flower.

speaker: NURSE
text: Nay, he’s a flower, in faith, a very flower.

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: What say you? Can you love the gentleman?

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: This night you shall behold him at our feast.

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: Read o’er the volume of young Paris’ face,

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: And find delight writ there with beauty’s pen.

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: Examine every married lineament

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: And see how one another lends content,

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: And what obscured in this fair volume lies

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: Find written in the margent of his eyes.

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: This precious book of love, this unbound lover,

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: To beautify him only lacks a cover.

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: The fish lives in the sea, and ’tis much pride

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: For fair without the fair within to hide.

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: That book in many’s eyes doth share the glory

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: That in gold clasps locks in the golden story.

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: So shall you share all that he doth possess

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: By having him, making yourself no less.

speaker: NURSE
text: No less? Nay, bigger. Women grow by men.

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: Speak briefly. Can you like of Paris’ love?

speaker: JULIET
text: I’ll look to like, if looking liking move.

speaker: JULIET
text: But no more deep will I endart mine eye

speaker: JULIET
text: Than your consent gives strength to make it fly.

speaker: SERVINGMAN
text: Madam, the guests are come, supper

speaker: SERVINGMAN
text: served up, you called, my young lady asked for, the

speaker: SERVINGMAN
text: Nurse cursed in the pantry, and everything in

speaker: SERVINGMAN
text: extremity. I must hence to wait. I beseech you,

speaker: SERVINGMAN
text: follow straight.

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: We follow thee.

speaker:
LADY
               
              CAPULET
text: Juliet, the County stays.

speaker: NURSE
text: Go, girl, seek happy nights to happy days.
